desc: A collided enemy can respawn overlapping the player, causing repeated score increments on subsequent frames without a new player action.
Player movement uses a fixed distance per frame instead of elapsed time, so game speed changes when the actual frame rate drops or varies.
Applying SPEED independently on both axes makes diagonal movement approximately 41% faster than horizontal or vertical movement.
Using elif gives LEFT and UP priority when opposite direction keys are held simultaneously instead of canceling the corresponding movement.
drawEverything constructs a new font object every frame, causing unnecessary repeated resource allocation in the main rendering loop.
initGame appends enemies without clearing enemyList, so calling it more than once accumulates additional enemies.
initGame does not reset runningGame, so the game loop exits immediately if the game is initialized again after a previous run.
Mutable game state is spread across module globals, tightly coupling initialization, updates, rendering, and collision handling and making reuse and testing difficult.
endGame unconditionally calls sys.exit, preventing callers from cleaning up or continuing execution when the module is embedded or tested.
